ai_setup_intro: Choose how SeekerMail should run AI features. You can change this at any time in Settings.
ai_setup_use_recommended_desc: One-click connection to a vetted provider with a no-train, no-retention policy. No API key needed.
ai_setup_use_my_key_desc: Bring an existing key from any supported provider and configure it yourself.
ai_setup_use_local_desc: Run AI entirely on this device. Nothing ever leaves your computer.
ai_setup_tier_intro: Both plans bill through your own provider account — SeekerMail never sits between you and the provider, and never marks up usage.
ai_setup_balanced_desc: Recommended. A capable mid-tier model balancing speed, quality, and cost.
ai_setup_high_quality_desc: A flagship model for the best drafts and analysis, at a higher cost.
ai_setup_authorizing: Waiting for authorization in your browser…
ai_setup_authorizing_hint: Complete the sign-in in the browser window that just opened. You will be brought back here automatically.
ai_setup_manual_code_hint: If the browser did not bring you back, paste the authorization code shown by the provider.
ai_setup_ready_desc: {provider} is connected. AI replies are now available across your accounts.
ai_setup_quota_notice: For the first 7 days, a conservative quota applies: 100 cloud calls per day and 2,000 tokens per reply. You can lift it in Settings.
ai_setup_error_generic: The provider could not be connected.
ai_setup_disclosure_body: To generate replies and analysis, SeekerMail will send the content of the emails you act on — plus relevant context retrieved from your local index — directly from this device to {provider}.
ai_setup_disclosure_point_direct: Requests go straight to the provider's endpoint. SeekerMail's servers are never on the path and can never see your mail.
ai_setup_disclosure_point_no_train: Recommended providers are selected for an explicit no-train, no-retention contractual stance.
ai_setup_disclosure_point_revoke: You can disconnect at any time in Settings; your token is stored only in the system keychain.
